_publications._publicationTitle: C-Fiber Loss as a Possible Cause of Neuropathic Pain in Schwannomatosis.

_contextSnippet: In the painDETECT, six of 20 patients scored in the upper range (19–38 points), which is indicative of a neuropathic pain syndrome. Seven patients had low scores (0–12 points) and, thus, showed no symptoms typically associated with neuropathic pain.
details: Six of 20 patients scored in the upper range (19–38 points), indicative of neuropathic pain syndrome. Seven patients had low scores (0–12 points) showing no neuropathic pain symptoms. Seven patients reached mid-range scores (13–18 points) allowing no precise classification.
